‘The new and improved accommodation
tor the Press at Croke Park is exciting
favourable comment in allquarters. With
a freeand unimpeded view of the enclosure,
the seribbling fraternity have at last “ got
on” to a good thing.

On Sunday last at Newbliss, Monaghan
footballers captured the Ulster champion-
ship from Fermanagh in decisive feshion,
Monaghan have presented a united front
this season, and, freed from internal dissen-
sion, they have meritoriously bustled their
way through to the top. Other counties

+ mightkeep the moral in view. Congratu-
Tations !

Since 1908 the Antrim footballers won
the Ulster championship without a break
until this season, when they did not survive
even the first round. of the competition.
Eis is typiccl-of-the-ups-and-downs-of
Gaelic football. In 1911 and 1912
Antti survived the inter-provincial tests,
and contested the All Ireland finals against
Cork any Louth respectively. They were
fot successful in gaining the verdict in
er battle, but their efforts bore good

‘as from that period may be dated the
pulse which has beought Ulster football
1 its present improved position.

— Secietary McGrath, of the Munster
‘Council, has, in his usual energetic fashion,
“risen superior to the hatdicaps with which
‘war outbreak seemed likely to cn

fmber him. In the halcyon days of peace
‘had arranged for the furling sea-finals

ay

tactics, even if they don’t succeed in out-
playing them. But what odds on a double
victory for Ulare ?

What about the Leinster and Munster
athletic championship meetings which
proved so successful in past seasons ? These
sporting attractions were formerly promoted
by the Leinster and Munster Provincial
Councils, and they generally provided fine
atbletic fare. Even at the tail-end of the
season it is not too late to bring off these
attractive competitions, and there is no rea
son to believe that they will not prove as
successful as of old

Cork and Kilkenny are famous hurling
counties, but in both there has been a little
internal friction of late, which, happily,
shows signs of abating. In Cork the hutling
inal lies between Midleton and Blackrock,
and-owing to a difference of opinion with
the County Board it seemed as though
Blackrock were to receive a bloodless vie
tory. Now, however, the game has been
re-fixed, and it seems likely that cheers
will ring out ‘The principal
parties tothe Kilkenny dispute were the
famous combinations which have figured
so largely in recent All-Ireland finals—
‘allaroan and Mooncoin. A sporting com-
promise has now been reached, and the op-
ponents will again meet in friendly rivalry.
Perhaps, afterall, such thingsare sent to try
us, and to make us conform to discipline,

at the present time to inclule events of

of negligence, If the county boafds are
going to fail in their duty in this respect,
some very drastic measures will need to be
adopted to have the interests of athletics
‘and. cycling protected by the G.A.A. If
these executive bodies finally decide that
they will confine their attention to football
and hurling, athletic and cycling county
boards will need to be formed without any
further experimenting ot trifling with the
subject, It is to be hoped that somebody
with the supieme interests of the Associa-
tion at hears, and whose personal disposition
shows no bias towards either football,
hurling, athletes oF cycling, will bring this
matter forward at the next Annual Con~
ress, and thus strike one blow to retrieve
four athletic prestige befure it becomes a
mere memory of beiter days.

ATHLETIC UNIFORMITY

EDITORIAL.
SESS

For the present, at all events, the European
war has ceased to have an’ adverse effect
upon Gaelic fixtures. The demands made
by the military upon the rolling stock of
the railway companies has lessened, and
patrons of Gaelic games are now enabled
to secure that efficient transport_accommo-

es to make our fixtures suc-

i

cessful. On Sunday next, those frequently-
disturbed fixtures, the Munster hurling
ff at Mallow,

companies are important factors in G.A.A,

operations, and we are, therefore, justified in

enced by the Association.

-_ Ff +

In another way, however, a deleterious
from which recovery may be somewhat
slower. cis complained that the Gaelic
shave been so cut up by cavalry
who have had their grounds damaged in this.
having their property repaired. They should
keep in view also the loss in revenue sus-
tained by fixtures being diverted elsewhere
during the period in which their grounds
ave passing through a recuperative stage
This is by no means an unreasonable view
of the matter, and it should be pressed home
‘opon the military authorities responsible for
the damage occasioned

- + +

The 1914 sports season has now only
another six or eight weeks of active life, but
we hope to see these utilised ta some-etect

‘A WORLD-WIDE MOVEMENT

The following general rules have been
adopted by the International Amatuer
Federation, and may be regarced as stand-
ards of conduct in matters athletic :-

In all international races of distance up
to and including 400 meters, each compet
itor shall have a seperate course measured
the full length properly roped (white) and
staked where the race is running on a
straight path or around one ar more curves.

Each competitor shall keep in his respect
ition from start to finish and all races
On races with
fe turns he shall not
al one until he is two meters
in advance of his nearest competitor.

No attendant shall accompany on the
mark or in the race, nor shall any compet
itor be allowed without the permission of
the Referee or Judges, to receive asistance

or refreshment from anyone. during the

Any competitor wilfully jostling or run=

ig another compet
ess shall for-
mpetition, and
bbe awarded any position or

iad otherwise been entitled to,

No competitor shall be allowed to rejoin
pose of gaining a place of to pace or assist
another competitor.

In all weight events the competitor must
stay in the circle till his attempt is marked

Tn all weight events, thrown from the

c may touch the circle,

and the circle is measured from the outside.

The circle is to be of metal, wood or
cope, 7 feet in diameter. In the middle of
the circumference, for shot putting, at the
front half of the circle shall be placed a
bop board 4 feet long, 4 inches high, which
shall be firmly fastened in the ground,

_MONAGHAN'S

BIG WIN

ALL FINAL

Exciting Connacht Tie.

Ak At Newbliss on Sunday Monaghan car-
ed off the honours in the final othe
Uister Football - Championship, beating
fetmanagh by a large score. No previous
inal in the Ulster province evoked such
widespread interest owing (o the fact that
‘few weeks previously Fermanagh Knocked
‘out Cavan County team, which had. de-
feated Antrim and Derry in fixtures played
this year and were regacded by followers of
the sport as the choice of Uliter in this
year's inter-county fixtures, Fermanagh
upset all calculations, but the fact remained
“that Monaghan stood a good chance of
forging its way to the Anal, from its recent
display with Armagh in the last of the
semistinals, when the two teams at first
pated with a scoreless draw
and inthe second Monaghan won by
& solitary. point. . Interest in Sundays
final was such that the crowd was huge, to
say the least, and were amply rewarded,
Mr. J, Coleman, Dungacvaa, bandled the
teams, and everything was in apple-pie
order under the field management of Mr. P.
Whelan J.P, President of the Ulster Coun
cilof the G.A.A., Mr. O'Duffy, the ener-
etic Secretary, Mr. O. Conlon and other
members of the Ulster Council

Title,

Roscommon

Is Connacht Hurling Championship.

Roscommon and Galway met in the final
of Connacht hurling championship at Ros-
‘common on Sunday last, before a very small
gathering of spectators. Galway were short
some of their best men, owing to an error
with regard to train arrangements, The
Loughrea mea wre unable to travel con-
sequently, and four subs had to fill up the

Te was long after the advertised time be-
fore the Galway team came on to the field.
The Roscommon players were fully an hour
on the field waiting, and it was 4 p.m.

when the following lined up :—
Roscommoa—J J Monahan (captain), H
and T Mouabao, M Nolan, J and M O'Braon-
ain, P Gillane,, P Conboy, E and P Kile
martin, B Conno Doyle, P Fingeran, P
Geraghty, P F Gillaue (goal)
1y (captain), Gand T
Morrisay, P O'Connor, T Gibboas P Hurnoy,

OConbor (goal), J Hanify, PJ Cooney, B
I McGlynn, J Molloy, B Nestor, N
nd D Malloy:

Mr. B. Murphy, Athlone, refereed.
Galway Defending

Rooney,

Galway won the toss and took the slight
advantage there was in the field. Roscom-
mon at once broke away, and Gibbons
cleared. Kilmartin returned, and J. J.
Monaban sent softly over, and next mo-
‘ment Conboy opened the score with a point
for Roscommon. O'Connor brought off a
reat save from Connolly in the Galway
goal, and Kenny sent well out of danger.
A free to Galway relieved the pressure.
Hannify sending to touch, Nestor had a
try at the other end, but O'Brennan care
to the rescue, Gillane bringing off a clever
save a moment later. A 70 yards fell to
Galway. Kenny being entrusted with the
puck, sent well into goal, H. Monahan
being fouled, From the free Roscommon
got going, and J. O'Brennan had a grand
chance, but sent weakly into Connor, who
cleared: Hanify was now working well
for his side but later on seemed to fall
away. -M. O'Brennan was very sound at
back, and Kilmartin and Gillane were doing,
splen Galway showed weakness at
centrefield, and Roscommon was very mo-
derate forward, missing several good chances
to score, Gereghty having hard lines. with
‘good shot. Roscommon had slightly the
best of matters up to this, J. J. Monahan

gaining a 70 for his side. J. O'Brennan

Monaghan team, though never hard
pressed, were in the pink of form and
Superior in every way to the combination
of Fermanagh. While it would be too
much to expect Monaghan
place of Wexford against Kerry in
Ireland final, it seems apparcot that Wex
ford won't ‘bave matters all its own way
